title: Extraction and detection of free-gossypol in cottonseed samples
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"cmu9i","text":"This simple and rapid chromatographic detection method was developed and validated in order to accurately quantify trace levels of free gossypol in different cotton materials, including cottonseed, cottonseed cake and cottonseed cake treated with macrofungi.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Dry the cottonseed sample at 40 °C for 24 hours. 24:00:00, hours This protocol was tested with samples from cottonseed with linter, cottonseed without linter, cottonseed cake and cottonseed cake treated wuith basidiomycetes fungi. Undetermined The sample should be very well dried in order to proceed to the next step
Grind the dry sample to form a fine powder with an ultra centrifugal mill (0.5 mm) the samples can be grinded using bench mill if it is very well homogenized
Weight 1 gram of the powdered sample into a 15mL plastic centrifuge tube with screw. undetermined
Add 3 mL of ultrapure water into the tube. undetermined Use Mili-Q water
Vortex vigorously for about 10 seconds. Make sure that the solution is very well homogenized. 00:00:10, seconds
Ultra-sonicate for 5 minutes using ice in the sonicator. 00:05:00, minutes The ice must fill the sonicator
Add 7 mL of acetone onto the mixture. undetermined Use grade HPLC reagent Acetone Contributed by users
Ultra-sonicate for 5 minutes using ice in the sonicator. The ice must fill the sonicator 00:05:00, minutes
Vortex vigorously for about 10 seconds. 00:00:10, seconds Make sure that the solution is very well homogenized
Centrifuge the tubes at 9000 rpm for 5 minutes at 8 °C. 00:05:00, minutes Undetermined
Transfer 2 mL of the supernatant to 15 mL centrifuge tube. undetermined
Evaporate the solvent in a speed vacuum at room temperature. All solvent must be evaporated The temperature should not be over 35ºC

Resuspend the residue with 200 µL of 70% acetone. undetermined 70% acetone Contributed by users Prepare 70% acetone using ultrapure water. The pellet remaining in the tube must be entirely suspended with the solvent.
Transfer the solution to a 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube. undetermined
Centrifuge at 14000 rpm for 10 minutes at 8 °C. 00:10:00, minutes Undetermined
Transfer the supernatant to vial.
Set a reversed phase C18 column (100 x 2.1 mm, 2.6 µm) in a UHPLC equipped with a photodiode array (PDA).
Set column temperature to 35 °C. Undetermined
Use methanol as organic solvent (A) and 0.1% TFA in water as aqueous solvent (B) for mobile phase constituents. Methanol Contributed by users Water 0.1% TFA Contributed by users

Set elution as gradient. The elution starts with 60% of A, changing linearly to 100% of A , it is reached at 8 minutes and maintained until 10 minutes. After this, the mobile phase suddenly changes to 60% of A, maintaining this isocratic flow till the end of run at 14 minutes. %s %s Time (min) %s %s Aqueous (%) %s %s Organic (%) %s %s Initial %s %s 40 %s %s 60 %s %s 8 %s %s 0 %s %s 100 %s %s 10 %s %s 0 %s %s 100 %s %s 10.01 %s %s 40 %s %s 60 %s %s 14 %s %s 40 %s %s 60 %s %s
Set the sample manage to 8 °C. Undetermined
Set the PDA monitoring to 254 nm.
Inject 2 µl of the sample. undetermined The gossypol peak should appear at 6.3 minutes using Empower software for analysis of chromatogram
Set the flow rate to 0.4 mL/min.
creator: Simone Mendonça
